Position: Executive, Shared Mobility
Agency: Ministry of Transport
Location: Singapore

About the role:
The Ministry of Transport is seeking a committed Executive, Shared Mobility to join our team. In this role you will
contribute to shared mobility, micro-mobility and active-transport programmes, working within a multidisciplinary group of professionals who
are passionate about delivering high-quality public services. You will be at the centre
of planning, executing and reviewing initiatives that have a direct and lasting impact
on the daily lives of citizens. The role combines hands-on delivery with strategic
thinking, and offers significant scope to shape how we approach shared mobility, micro-mobility and active-transport programmes in the years ahead.

Key responsibilities:
  - Lead and support bus and rail integration across the division, coordinating with internal teams and external partners to deliver measurable outcomes.
  - Lead and support bike-sharing licensing across the division, coordinating with internal teams and external partners to deliver measurable outcomes.
  - Lead and support first-and-last-mile connectivity across the division, coordinating with internal teams and external partners to deliver measurable outcomes.
  - Prepare clear written reports, briefing notes and presentations for senior management
    and external stakeholders, translating technical detail into actionable recommendations.
  - Monitor relevant metrics and trends, proactively identifying risks and opportunities
    and proposing well-reasoned, evidence-based courses of action.
  - Build and maintain strong working relationships with partner agencies, industry bodies
    and members of the public, representing the Ministry of Transport with professionalism and integrity.
  - Continuously review and improve processes, applying lessons learned to raise the quality,
    efficiency and resilience of our work.

Requirements:
  - A relevant degree or equivalent professional qualification, with demonstrated experience
    in a comparable field; candidates from adjacent disciplines with transferable skills are
    welcome to apply.
  - Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to work independently and
    manage competing priorities under tight timelines.
  - Excellent written and verbal communication skills, and a genuine commitment to public service.
  - A collaborative mindset, sound judgement, and a track record of seeing initiatives through
    from concept to completion.

What we offer:
  - A meaningful career with the opportunity to make a tangible difference to the community.
  - Structured training, mentorship and clear pathways for professional development and growth.
  - A supportive, inclusive and forward-looking work environment that values diverse perspectives.
  - Competitive remuneration and a comprehensive package of employee benefits.

How to apply:
Interested applicants should submit a detailed resume together with a short cover letter
outlining their suitability for the role. Only shortlisted candidates will be notified.
The Ministry of Transport is an equal-opportunity employer and welcomes applications from all backgrounds.

Location: Singapore

About the role:
The Ministry of Health is seeking a committed Epidemiologist to join our team. In this role you will
contribute to communicable-disease surveillance and outbreak analysis, working within a multidisciplinary group of professionals who
are passionate about delivering high-quality public services. You will be at the centre
of planning, executing and reviewing initiatives that have a direct and lasting impact
on the daily lives of citizens. The role combines hands-on delivery with strategic
thinking, and offers significant scope to shape how we approach communicable-disease surveillance and outbreak analysis in the years ahead.

Key responsibilities:
  - Lead and support statistical modelling across the division, coordinating with internal teams and external partners to deliver measurable outcomes.
  - Lead and support outbreak investigation across the division, coordinating with internal teams and external partners to deliver measurable outcomes.
  - Lead and support policy advisory across the division, coordinating with internal teams and external partners to deliver measurable outcomes.
  - Prepare clear written reports, briefing notes and presentations for senior management
    and external stakeholders, translating technical detail into actionable recommendations.
  - Monitor relevant metrics and trends, proactively identifying risks and opportunities
    and proposing well-reasoned, evidence-based courses of action.
  - Build and maintain strong working relationships with partner agencies, industry bodies
    and members of the public, representing the Ministry of Health with professionalism and integrity.
  - Continuously review and improve processes, applying lessons learned to raise the quality,
    efficiency and resilience of our work.

Requirements:
  - A relevant degree or equivalent professional qualification, with demonstrated experience
    in a comparable field; candidates from adjacent disciplines with transferable skills are
    welcome to apply.
  - Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to work independently and
    manage competing priorities under tight timelines.
  - Excellent written and verbal communication skills, and a genuine commitment to public service.
  - A collaborative mindset, sound judgement, and a track record of seeing initiatives through
    from concept to completion.

What we offer:
  - A meaningful career with the opportunity to make a tangible difference to the community.
  - Structured training, mentorship and clear pathways for professional development and growth.
  - A supportive, inclusive and forward-looking work environment that values diverse perspectives.
  - Competitive remuneration and a comprehensive package of employee benefits.

How to apply:
Interested applicants should submit a detailed resume together with a short cover letter
outlining their suitability for the role. Only shortlisted candidates will be notified.
The Ministry of Health is an equal-opportunity employer and welcomes applications from all backgrounds.
